NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel deposition testimony
Granted in part and denied in part. The Court has read and considered the late-filed opposition. The witness is ordered to answer the following questions as set forth in the separate statement of matters in dispute: 1, 3-7, 21, 25, and 65. The Court finds that the answers to the specified questions could not possibly tend to incriminate the witness. The invocation of the privilege is proper as to the remaining questions. The Court further orders that the witness is barred from giving testimony at trial, except to assert the Fifth Amendment, until and unless he makes himself available for deposition.
